title: Thelypteris grisea

Its classification helps to distinguish it from other similar fern species.

intro: Thelypteris grisea, also known as the gray polypody, is a species of fern native to Central and South America, characterized by its small, lance-shaped fronds and grayish-green color. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, wounds, and infections. Thelypteris grisea contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
